activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: All swimmers have to wear a cap in this pool.
activities.explanation: «Swimmers have to wear» forma correctamente una obligación con sujeto plural.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: My sister have to renew her library card.
activities.explanation: El sujeto singular requiere «has to renew».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: You must keep your receipt.
activities.explanation: «Must» va seguido correctamente de la forma base «keep».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: Employees must to lock the office at night.
activities.explanation: Después de «must» no se usa «to»: «must lock».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence expresses a prohibition.
activities.prompt: Passengers mustn't open the doors while the bus is moving.
activities.explanation: «Mustn't open» indica que abrir las puertas está prohibido.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the question is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: Does Aisha has to collect the keys before six?
activities.explanation: Después de «Does» debe aparecer la forma base: «have to collect».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the question is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: Do cyclists have to use this lane?
activities.explanation: La pregunta plural usa correctamente «Do cyclists have to».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence correctly expresses past obligation.
activities.prompt: The storm damaged the roof, so we had to cancel the class.
activities.explanation: «Had to cancel» expresa una obligación causada por una situación pasada.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence correctly expresses past obligation.
activities.prompt: Yesterday, the technicians musted restart the system.
activities.explanation: «Musted» no existe; una obligación pasada usa «had to restart».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence correctly expresses future obligation.
activities.prompt: Next season, the club will have to replace the floodlights.
activities.explanation: «Will have to replace» es la forma normal de obligación futura.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence matches the stated situation: borrowing a helmet is optional.
activities.prompt: You don't have to borrow a helmet from reception; you can use your own.
activities.explanation: «Don't have to» permite elegir, y la segunda cláusula ofrece una alternativa.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence matches the stated situation: photography is forbidden.
activities.prompt: You don't have to take photographs in the conservation room.
activities.explanation: «Don't have to» significa que no es necesario; para una prohibición se necesita «mustn't».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence matches the stated situation: lunch is provided, so bringing food is unnecessary.
activities.prompt: Participants mustn't bring lunch because the organiser provides it.
activities.explanation: La comida proporcionada elimina la necesidad, pero no implica prohibición: «don't have to bring».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: You won't have to queue once the new ticket gates open.
activities.explanation: «Won't have to queue» expresa correctamente ausencia de necesidad futura.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the past question is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: Did the players have to arrive two hours early?
activities.explanation: La pregunta pasada usa «Did + sujeto + have to + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence accurately communicates the notice: “Entry beyond this point is forbidden.”
activities.prompt: Visitors mustn't go beyond this point.
activities.explanation: «Mustn't go» comunica una prohibición directa.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence accurately communicates the notice: “Using the cloakroom is optional.”
activities.prompt: Visitors mustn't use the cloakroom.
activities.explanation: Una opción se expresa con «don't have to use»; «mustn't» la prohibiría.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the indirect question is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: Could you tell me if contractors have to sign in every morning?
activities.explanation: La pregunta indirecta mantiene el orden «contractors have to».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the indirect question is grammatically correct.
activities.prompt: Do you know whether does Mina have to attend the briefing?
activities.explanation: No hay inversión dentro de la pregunta indirecta: «whether Mina has to attend».

activities.instructions: Decide whether both time forms are correct.
activities.prompt: We had to submit monthly reports last year, but next year we won't have to submit them.
activities.explanation: «Had to» marca obligación pasada y «won't have to» ausencia de necesidad futura.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence is logically consistent.
activities.prompt: You mustn't upload the file, but uploading it is entirely optional.
activities.explanation: «Mustn't» prohíbe la acción; una acción opcional está permitida pero no es obligatoria.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence is logically consistent.
activities.prompt: You don't have to join the guided tour, so you may explore the public rooms independently.
activities.explanation: La segunda opción confirma que la visita guiada no es obligatoria.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence correctly combines a past requirement and present no-necessity.
activities.prompt: We had to carry paper maps on earlier trips, but we don't have to carry them now.
activities.explanation: El contraste usa correctamente «had to» en el pasado y «don't have to» en el presente.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence correctly combines future obligation and passive form.
activities.prompt: From July, every imported device will have to be tested locally.
activities.explanation: «Will have to be tested» combina obligación futura con una pasiva correcta.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence correctly reports no necessity in the past.
activities.prompt: The hotel offered free transfers, so guests didn't had to arrange a taxi.
activities.explanation: Después de «didn't» se usa la forma base: «didn't have to arrange».
